sections.title: Overview
sections.content: The RACE scale is a tool used by paramedics and other prehospital personnel to quickly assess patients with suspected stroke and determine if they have a large vessel occlusion (LVO), a major cause of stroke. The scale includes five criteria, facial palsy, arm motor impairment, leg motor impairment, head and gaze deviation and aphasia-agnosia, The RACE scale has a higher sensitivity than other simpler scales.
